Type: B2B dev tool — AI copilot + MCP server for building/debugging n8n workflows (Next/TS/Anthropic/GCP); YC-backed
Verdict: OUT OF BUDGET — MARGINAL on merits (57/100)
One-line reason: A genuine, revenue-verified business (~$12.2k MRR, 260 paying subs, ~$113k trailing-year, YC-backed) in the team's exact domain — but declining ~20% from its March peak with an eroding moat (n8n's own AI + free open-source n8n MCP servers) and a structural single-platform dependency, priced at $100,000 (~33x the $3k budget) with no financing: OUT OF BUDGET on merits it could otherwise pursue.

Type: Vertical B2B/prosumer micro-SaaS — financial + CPD dashboard for UK associate dentists
One-line reason: Cheap ($350, 1.6x) and Stripe-verified, with a real founder-partnership path — but as a solo asset-buy the value transfers away with the dentist (domain expertise + dental-community distribution), so value_transfers=false caps it at MARGINAL (provisional); worth pursuing only if the founder commits to partnering.

Right-shaped deal — a web app the team can fully own and scale, so operability scores high — but everything that would make it a business (users, retention, revenue, transferable IP) is undisclosed and unverifiable; completeness ~38% is too low to trust any tier, so v2 returns INSUFFICIENT DATA rather than the confident MARGINAL v1 implied.

Type: B2B micro-SaaS — no-code Telegram storefront builder (Node.js / Vue 3 / Docker)
One-line reason: Right type and stack (B2B micro-SaaS, Node/Vue/Docker, a genuinely growing Telegram-commerce market) but near-pre-revenue with essentially nothing verified — no MRR, customer count, retention, price, or URL — so completeness lands below 45% and v2 refuses a confident tier; the seller's own 'it needs marketing/distribution' (the team's weakness) and hard Telegram-platform lock-in are the standing red flags.

Type: B2B hospitality SaaS — QR/app at-table ordering & order management for restaurants/bars (web + iOS/Android), UK
One-line reason: Right shape (a real UK web+mobile hospitality SaaS you could operate) but the #1 growth lever is B2B restaurant/bar sales — your weakness — in a saturated market owned by POS incumbents (Toast/Square/SpotOn) and specialists (Sunday, me&u); it's order-management-only (no payments), shows no disclosed traction, and is over budget. growth_lever_fits=false → PASS (provisional, revenue unverified).

Type: Affiliate coupon site (AliExpress via Admitad, WordPress)
One-line reason: PASS: a single-program AliExpress affiliate/SEO coupon site whose only growth lever (content + SEO + audience work) is exactly this team's weakness — growth_lever_fits fails; 100% revenue-concentrated in one clawback-prone program, neglected/likely declining, in a category under attribution assault (Honey/extension hijacking). Cheap and cash-flowing but over the $3k budget — Pass on merits, so the budget flag is moot.

Type: Web/product analytics SaaS ('revenue-first' analytics + funnels + AI suggestions), Next/TS
One-line reason: Right shape (analytics SaaS in the team's wheelhouse, Stripe-verified revenue, in budget) but hobby-tier and flat — ~$40 MRR from 5 subs after ~7 months — in one of software's most saturated commodity markets with zero moat, priced at an expensive 6.3x, and its only real distribution is the founder's YouTube, which doesn't transfer; the growth lever (commodity + key-person channel) fails the team's fit gate → PASS.

Well-built and fast-growing, but growth is 100% SEO/content (the team's weakness — the growth-lever gate fails → PASS) in the most AI-Overview-exposed niche, with unproven week-old AdSense revenue and classic build-to-flip timing at the top of a 3-month ramp.

Type: Content blog (AI-generated) / storefront for a 'starter website' flipping operation (WordPress + custom auction plugin)
One-line reason: PASS (merit ~30): the clearest junk — a ~6-month-old, AI-generated content/starter-site flipping storefront with no revenue and no real audience (1 organic click on 1,053 impressions, 2 returning visitors, ~291 mostly bot/direct 'users'); growth_lever_fits=FALSE because the only path to customers is SEO/content, the team's weakness, and there is no real business to buy.

Type: micro-SaaS — AI wrapper (pitch-deck review, pay-per-credit), solo founder Nepal, founded Dec 2025
One-line reason: A zero-moat AI wrapper in a category where the seller's own named competitors (Slidebean, SeedBlink) give the product away free as lead-gen; $17k prices a 7-month launch spike at 17x last-month revenue, the credit flow is anti-retention by design, and the undisclosed acquisition channel plus a marketing-led growth plan sit squarely on this team's declared weakness.

Type: content/directory micro-site — paid ($49 LTD) database of AI-agent ideas (Next.js 15/Supabase/Stripe/Cloudflare)
Asking price: $2,000 (TrustMRR; '0.1x' = last-30d spike annualized — honest: ~0.82x all-time / ~2.1x true ARR)
One-line reason: First in-budget deal ($2k vs $3k) but a verified spike-and-flip: a 3-week-old domain sold as a 14-month business (whois 2026-06-27 vs claimed May-2025 founding), the $1,422 'spike month' is ~29 x $49 lifetime deals with $0 in the last 7 days, the '$78 MRR / 19 subs' cannot come from the site's own no-subscription pricing, the live site is fronted by a DIFFERENT person (@iamfra5er) than the seller (@mattmerrick16), and the only acquisition channel is the seller's personal X audience - which does not convey.

Asking price: $600 (TrustMRR; '0.9x' = one recent payment annualized — honest: ~9.3x all-time revenue of $64.59)
One-line reason: A 6-month-old Spotify-concert-finder with 1 paying subscriber and $65 all-time revenue, selling into a category Bandsintown (~100M users), Songkick and Spotify's own live-events feed already serve for free — growth would be pure consumer marketing (SEO/social), the team's stated weakness.

Type: micro-SaaS / B2B tool (AI invoice collection & reconciliation)
Asking price: $5,000 (TrustMRR; 1.3x stated ≈ 1.25x annualized MRR — arithmetic honest, but on 5 subs / $660 all-time)
One-line reason: The best-shaped target from TrustMRR so far — real B2B recurring revenue ($333 MRR, 5 subs, $29–79 tiers) in a category with a genuine EU e-invoicing regulatory tailwind — but 3 of its 5 showcase customers are the founder's own products, its stated growth channel is content/SEO the team can't run, and the $5,000 asking is 1.67x the budget with no financing.

Type: micro-SaaS / B2B tool (AI App Store screenshot generator)
Asking price: $5,000 (TrustMRR; cut from $9,000 within ~4 days of listing; '0.8x' = last-30d revenue annualized — vs 1.03x on MRR)
One-line reason: A 10-week-old AI screenshot generator with real early traction (30 subs, $405 MRR) being flipped at the top of its launch curve — episodic-need product with subscription pricing (MRR growth +7% vs revenue +51% = churn already showing), zero moat in a saturated category, distribution = the founder's X audience, and a $5k ask that is 1.67x budget after a 44% price cut in days.

Type: micro-SaaS / B2B tool (AI Reddit lead-gen & marketing platform)
Asking price: $5,000 (TrustMRR; '0.3x' = spike-month revenue annualized — honest: 0.53x on $782 MRR; reconciled, same annualization pattern as AgentIdeaHub/AppShots)
One-line reason: A 2-year-old Reddit lead-gen tool riding a spike from category-leader GummySearch's shutdown — but that shutdown IS the deal-killer: Reddit killed the leader over commercial API licensing and blocked unauthenticated access in May 2026, one month before this was listed; the seller's own reason for sale ('can't provide needed distribution') names the other kill gate, and $5k is 1.67x budget with 50 competing offers.

Type: micro-SaaS / B2B tool (local-business lead finder for web freelancers, Spanish-language markets)
Asking price: $7,500 (TrustMRR; 2.5x stated ≈ 2.3x on MRR-ARR — the highest multiple in the TrustMRR batch, on a 3-month-old product)
One-line reason: A 3-month-old Google-Maps lead scraper for Spanish-speaking web freelancers asking 2.5x annualized on $272 MRR (2.5x the budget) — commodity category with entrenched incumbents, distribution is the founder's Instagram, ARPU ($9.71) sits below the listing's own cheapest tier because the live site's pricing doesn't match the listing, and the long-term pain it monetizes ('businesses without websites') is being shrunk by AI site builders.
